events.title: ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan
events.description: The UN’s top human rights official has issued an urgent warning that an imminent offensive against El Obeid, the capital of Sudan’s North Kordofan state, carries the risk of serious international crimes and threatens to deepen an already catastrophic humanitarian crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan

events.title: Colombian military neutralizes five terrorists accused in bus bombing that killed 20, injured 45 civilians
events.description: Colombian military forces neutralized five members of the militant group allegedly responsible for the bus bombing that killed 20 civilians in Cauca.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Colombian military neutralizes five terrorists accused in bus bombing that killed 20, injured 45 civilians

events.title: Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk
events.description: Abdiqadir Salah was pierced by shrapnel in a bombing that killed 12 in Somalia. But as the US denies civilians were hurt they face no hope of compensation Read more: Killed walking home from school: why did Somali children become targets of US drone strikes? A seven-year-old boy who was riddled with shrapnel during a deadly US airstrike in Somalia faces losing his ability to walk unless he has a £750 emergency operation. But Abdiqadir Salah’s family cannot afford the surgery and the US – which refuses to admit that any civilians were killed or injured during its attack six months ago – appears unwilling to pay compensation to those affected by airstrikes in Somalia. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk

events.title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
events.description: The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in early September.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean

events.title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children
events.description: Drought, extreme heat and heatwaves are the most prevalent trio of hazards endangering millions of children globally, warned a newly released climate report by the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children

events.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
events.description: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the world. The officials said the outbreak, now the third largest on record, required “strong immediate support”, but that the global risk remained low. Continue reading...

events.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
events.description: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ design Canadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft. The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the Atlantic Ocean in June 2023 en route to the wreckage of the Titanic ocean liner. But nearly two hours after it departed with five passengers, communications went dark. The disappearance prompted a frantic international search, with Canada and the US marshalling all available resources . Continue reading...

events.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
events.description: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed

events.title: Hate speech is ‘first step down the path of dehumanisation’
events.description: As online platforms continue to fuel a surge in real-world violence against vulnerable communities, UN Secretary-General António Guterres is warning that freedom of expression must never be used to justify hate speech.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hate speech is ‘first step down the path of dehumanisation’

events.title: US military kills three 'narco-terrorists' in latest lethal strike on vessel in the Eastern Pacific
events.description: U.S. military carried out a lethal strike on a vessel in the Eastern Pacific, killing three men described as narco-terrorists, according to SOUTHCOM.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military kills three 'narco-terrorists' in latest lethal strike on vessel in the Eastern Pacific

events.title: Worsening hunger could push millions closer to famine in 13 global hotspots
events.description: Millions of people across some of the world’s most vulnerable regions are expected to face worsening hunger in the months ahead, as conflict, economic pressures and shrinking aid budgets exacerbate long-running crises.

events.title: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
events.description: Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on women, peace and security
events.description: The UN Security Council met in New York Wednesday for its annual open debate on women, peace and security, in line with a 25-year-old resolution. The Secretary-General’s most recent report on the issue shows that in 2024, women made up only seven percent of negotiators on average worldwide, and nearly nine out of 10 negotiation tracks included no women negotiators at all. Follow live coverage below, and for daily coverage, check out our full meetings coverage here.

events.title: UN urges adherence to mine ban treaty
events.description: Countries must uphold international law limiting the use of anti-personnel mines, which kill and maim civilians long after conflicts have ended, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Tuesday.

events.title: Explosions and sustained gunfire reported at Niger airport
events.description: Soldiers have been searching for militants around the main airport and military airbase in Niger's capital Niamey.

events.title: Ebola cases increase almost 40% in a week as death toll passes 200
events.description: The Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda has claimed 204 deaths, with 894 confirmed cases, Africa’s Centres for Disease Control and Prevention said
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Ebola cases increase almost 40% in a week as death toll passes 200

events.title: Niger security forces battle gunmen at airport
events.description: Niger's security forces engaged in an hours-long shootout with gunmen at the nation's main international airport. No one has taken responsibility but Islamists are suspected of being behind the attack.

events.title: Lebanon: 12 children killed, maimed daily, despite Hezbollah-Israel truce
events.description: More than 100 days of war reveals a shattering snapshot of life for civilians in Lebanon amid Israeli strikes and forced displacement, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) reported on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon: 12 children killed, maimed daily, despite Hezbollah-Israel truce

events.title: World News in Brief: Risky return home in Lebanon, displacement in Gaza, emergency funding for Somalia
events.description: Despite the agreement announced on Sunday between the United States and Iran, displaced people in Lebanon continue to face difficulties in returning to their communities, the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has reported.

events.title: World News in Brief: Reduced violence in Lebanon, shortages in Gaza, rising debt impacts development funding
events.description: Although United Nations peacekeepers continue to observe violence and exchanges of fire in Lebanon, the level is significantly reduced when compared to the weekend, UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Tuesday.

events.title: Resettlement remains out of reach for millions of refugees
events.description: As millions of people continue to flee their countries due to war or persecution, UNHCR, the UN refugee agency, called on Tuesday for an urgent expansion of refugee resettlement programmes, warning that available places continue to fall short of global needs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Resettlement remains out of reach for millions of refugees

events.title: World Cup 2026: Mexico becomes first country to reach knockout stage after beating South Korea
events.description: Co-hosts Mexico became the first team to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage after a 1-0 victory over South Korea at a jubilant Guadalajara Stadium on Thursday. Luis Romo scored shortly after half-time, capitalising on a goalkeeping error, to seal Mexico's second straight win and confirm them as Group A winners with one match remaining.

events.title: Canada - Qatar: Relive Les Rouges' first World Cup win in dominant display
events.description: Canada produced a dominant display to dismantle Qatar 6-0 in their Group B match on Thursday, taking full control after a chaotic second half in which the visitors collapsed both structurally and numerically. Follow the key moments from the match below.

events.title: Did a Hitler lookalike really attend Germany's World Cup game?
events.description: The World Cup is well underway across North America, bringing its usual mix of spectacular goals, historic upsets and colourful stadium crowds. But alongside the genuine celebrations, a wave of manipulated media is flooding social media. From a fake Hitler lookalike to hyper-realistic female football enthusiasts designed exclusively to bait clicks, France 24's Maya-Anaïs Yataghène investigates these viral supporters in this edition of Truth or Fake.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Did a Hitler lookalike really attend Germany's World Cup game?

events.title: Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network
events.description: Young adults and teens are being recruited through apps like Telegram and paid to carry out attacks, officials say Police investigators in Toronto have said that dozens of shootings – including one at the US consulate in March – are linked to a “multilayered” gun-for-hire network that is also responsible for attacks on synagogues around Canada’s largest city. Toronto’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, told reporters on Tuesday that young adults and teenagers are being recruited through encrypted messaging apps such as Signal, Telegram and WhatsApp by “bad actors” and paid by the networks to carry out the attacks. Shooters are required to film their attacks in order to get paid. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network

events.title: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
events.description: The head of the UN-affiliated atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency

events.title: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report
events.description: Mexican authorities found a decomposing corpse with signs of violence near Tijuana's Caliente Stadium, where Iran's team trains for the World Cup.

events.title: US-Iran talks: Hope and skepticism in the Swiss alps
events.description: World leaders welcomed an end to fighting and relief from a global energy crunch — but critics doubt a lasting deal is in sight. Will history be made in Switzerland?

events.title: US launches Germany pharma pricing probe, raising threat of new tariffs
events.description: Germany came under fresh US trade scrutiny on Thursday as the United States launched an investigation into the country's pharmaceutical pricing policies, a move that could pave the way for new tariffs. Washington said it would examine whether Germany's pricing practices unfairly underpay for innovative medicines and burden US commerce under Section 301 trade authorities.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Manzambi's brace powers Switzerland past Bosnia
events.description: Switzerland put on a devastating display at the Sofi Stadium in Los Angeles, overwhelming Bosnia-Herzegovina 4-1 after a stunning second-half turnaround. With Bosnia down to ten men following an 80th-minute red card, substitutes Jovan Manzambi and Ruben Vargas ran riot. Ermin Mahmic's late strike provided only consolation for the Bosnians.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World Cup 2026: Manzambi's brace powers Switzerland past Bosnia

events.title: 'Pure hell' in Moscow as Ukrainian drones strike major refinery supplying capital's fuel market
events.description: Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks on Moscow, striking a major oil refinery and sending thick black smoke over parts of the Russian capital.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Pure hell' in Moscow as Ukrainian drones strike major refinery supplying capital's fuel market

events.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
events.description: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

events.title: Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC
events.description: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan ramping up defence is 'critical' to prevent war, Defence Minister Koizumi tells BBC

events.title: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital

events.title: Behind the noise of an ‘Iran deal’, Palestine continues to burn

events.title: Canada vs Qatar World Cup: 3 goals for David, 2 red cards, 1 injured Kone
events.description: BC Place was a battlefield as Kone was stretchered off, Qatar got two red cards, and both teams brawled after full-time.

events.title: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds
events.description: Review reveals rise in users and rates of psychosis in countries where cannabis is sold commercially Decriminalising the possession of cannabis or strictly regulating access to the drug do not appear to drive up usage, but when the drug is sold commercially the number of users increases and more mental health problems are seen, a review has found. An international team analysed the dramatic shift in policies on cannabis between 2000 and 2025, including how the numbers of people taking the drug, its potency, and rates of psychosis changed after new rules came in. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘Darkest chapter’: Record child violations in 2025, with national forces leading the way
events.description: For the first time, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups – and 2025 set a grim new record for the total number of child victims.

events.title: Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport
events.description: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Thirty-five killed as gunmen attack Niger's biggest airport

events.title: Protesters to rally against World Cup sponsor Hyundai before Mexico game
events.description: Focus on business dealings with mining company Guadalajara rally to highlight fate of ‘disappeared’ Hyundai will be targeted by protesters at a rally before the Group A game between Mexico and South Korea in Guadalajara on Thursday, due to the World Cup sponsor’s business dealings with the South American mining company Ternium. A 2025 report from the environmental group Mighty Earth criticised Hyundai’s involvement in what they described as a “dirty steel supply chain”, as the South Korean motor company is a major buyer of iron ore from Ternium for use in steel production. Ternium has faced repeated criticisms for its destructive environmental impact and corporate governance policies from campaign groups, as well as its alleged links to the disappearance of two Mexican activists. Continue reading...

events.title: Poland arrests a suspect in daylight killing of a Russian artist critical of Putin
events.description: Polish authorities have arrested a man suspected of fatally shooting a Russian activist critical of President Vladimir Putin
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Poland arrests a suspect in daylight killing of a Russian artist critical of Putin

events.title: Mexico advances in the World Cup and other top photos
events.description: June 12-18, 2026 Mexico became the first country to reach the knockout stage of the World Cup, beating South Korea 1-0. Merlín the duck is celebrated as the team mascot. Conservative Keiko Fujimori leads progressive Roberto Sánchez as votes are slowly counted from Peru’s presidential runoff election. A deeply divided electorate is preparing to vote for Colombia’s next president in a tight runoff election between a progressive and a conservative outsider. This gallery was curated by photo editor Anita Baca based in Mexico City. ___ AP photography: https://apnews.com/photography AP News on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/apnews

events.title: Global fish production hits new high, but climate threats loom
events.description: Aquaculture now supplies most of the fish people eat, a landmark report by the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) reveals, but rising temperatures and overfishing are putting the future of the industry at risk.

events.title: Russia linked to arson attacks on properties connected to UK PM Keir Starmer, police say
events.description: Russia allegedly linked to arson attacks on U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's properties, with a reported diplomat accused of recruiting Ukrainian nationals.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Ukraine urges EU to pressure Russia to end war before winter
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy say his country will need help if the war with Russia continues into the winter.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine urges EU to pressure Russia to end war before winter

events.title: Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high … of 1%
events.description: Country acts amid Iran war inflation pressures, but US Fed and Bank of England expected to hold rates Business live – latest updates The Bank of Japan (BoJ) has raised interest rates to a 31-year high as it tries to dampen inflationary pressures created by the Iran war. Policymakers in Tokyo raised the BoJ’s short-term policy rate by a quarter of one percentage point, to 1% from 0.75% , and warned that companies were passing on rising oil costs to each other at a “relatively fast pace”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high … of 1%

events.title: Australia news live: Chalmers dismisses reports of ‘frosty’ relationship with Albanese
events.description: Follow today’s news live • Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast There were two small earthquakes in New South Wales this morning, according to Geoscience Australia. A 3.4 magnitude quake hit near Orange around 5.45am this morning, with at least 108 people reporting they felt the shakes. And a 3.2 magnitude temblor hit near Wilcannia two hours earlier. Just two people reported feeling that quake. If we’re given the choice between taking the easier path politically or doing the more difficult thing, but the right thing in the interests of particularly first home buyers, young people, workers in the tax system, then it will be worth it. Obviously, there’s a big partisan political campaign against these changes. We understand that. We anticipated that. We expected that. But it’s worth it because our job isn’t just to take the easy political options. The more important thing than the politics of this is to get the policy right, to get the substance right. Because at the end of the day, what matters most is the tax system and the housing market are broken. We are acting to fix it. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Australia news live: Chalmers dismisses reports of ‘frosty’ relationship with Albanese

events.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
events.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack

events.title: Report: Russia's nuclear-powered 'Skyfall' missile is dirty and dangerous
events.description: MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."

events.title: FIFA hydration breaks have sparked criticism. But what do they actually do?

events.title: World Cup: Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi denied entry to Canada
events.description: Elephants striker Elye Wahi is being investigated in France in connection to allegations of match-fixing. The 23-year-old, who is contracted to Eintracht Frankfurt, is not the first player to be denied a Canadian visa.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World Cup: Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi denied entry to Canada

events.title: World Cup? Not today: New York is celebrating the Knicks
events.description: The FIFA World Cup may be under way in the United States, but in New York on Thursday, all eyes were on basketball. Tens of thousands of fans packed the streets of Manhattan to celebrate the NBA champion Knicks as the team paraded through the city under tight security. FRANCE 24’s Juliette Montilly was there.

events.title: About 300 children and teachers rescued after fire breaks out at Tokyo school
events.description: Officials say a fire broke out at an elementary school in downtown Tokyo, but that all of the roughly 300 students and teachers were evacuated or rescued

events.title: Monica Juma: Steering global cooperation at a time of converging threats
events.description: Criminal networks are moving faster, reaching further and blurring the lines between threats. Into this shifting landscape steps Monica Juma, taking on one of the UN’s most demanding security and justice portfolios.

events.title: DHS moves all detainees out of ‘Alligator Alcatraz’ amid hurricane concerns

events.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
events.description: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

events.title: US-Iran deal: Between conflict and compromise

events.title: NATO: US lashes out, alliance head says everything's fine

events.title: Middle East live: Israel pounds southern Lebanon, US-Iran talks postponed

The strikes threaten the nascent US-Iran interim agreement, which includes the cessation of hostilities in Lebanon. US-Iran talks scheduled for Friday in Switzerland have been postponed. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.

events.description: Knicks fans packed Manhattan on Thursday for a heavily secured victory parade celebrating the New York team's NBA Finals triumph, turning the city into a sea of blue and orange. Fans chanted "Let's go Knicks" as the procession moved through packed streets, with some paying hundreds of dollars for line sitters to secure prime viewing spots overnight.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
events.description: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

events.title: From camel coats to guochao: Max Mara woos China’s luxury brand consumers

Shortly before winning Farrer in May’s byelection – the first federal lower house seat One Nation has won outright – Farley said Labor’s current immigration intake of 306,000 was “probably not” too much . events.title: Conflation of Jewish identity with Israel driving antisemitism, Jewish Council says in submission to royal commission
events.description: Progressive Jewish group calls for more focus on the threat from the far right and the recognition of a diversity of views within the community Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Far-right extremism and the conflation of Jewish identity with Israel are the main drivers of antisemitism in Australia, the Jewish Council of Australia (JCA) says. In its submission to the royal commission on antisemitism and social cohesion, the liberal Jewish group calls for more focus on the “often overlooked” threat from the far right, and recognition of the diversity of views within the Jewish community instead of the “tendency to treat Jews collectively as representatives of Israel”. events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks

To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: Sierra Leone’s first lady refuses to condemn FGM without ‘reliable data’ on harms

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
events.description: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Jair Bolsonaro’s son sentenced to four years in jail for seeking US interference in father’s Brazil coup trial
events.description: Brazil supreme court finds that Eduardo Bolsonaro – who resides in the US - tried to get sanctions put on judges trying ex-president over coup plot Brazil’s supreme court has sentenced Eduardo Bolsonaro to four years and two months in prison after finding him guilty of courting US ⁠interference in his father’s coup plot trial last year. The office of Brazil’s ‌prosecutor general had ‌charged Eduardo Bolsonaro – who lives in the US - courting interference from the Trump administration to help Jair Bolsonaro’s case, by imposing sanctions on the court’s justices and tariffs on Brazilian goods. Continue reading...

events.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
events.description: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Initial testing at a WA laboratory returned a “suspected positive result for avian influenza”, the agriculture minister, Julie Collins, said. Continue reading...

events.title: UAE imposes limits on teen social media access
events.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has announced a social media ban for all children under 15. Platforms have one year to review and remove under-age accounts or face stiff penalties.

events.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
events.description: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
events.description: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

events.title: Oil nations on edge in the face of new climate coalition
events.description: Even amid an ongoing energy crunch, major economies are dodging firm timelines to phase out coal, oil and gas. Fossil fuel-producing nations now fear new clean energy alliances could ramp up the pressure to go green.

events.title: India news: Telegram is 'like Frankenstein,' government says
events.description: The platform — temporarily banned in India — has grown into a "monster," officials say. Elswhere, the air force is helping secure the NEET-UG retests, while Modi and Trump are "very close" to trade deal. DW has more.

events.title: Starmer rival Burnham wins Makerfield as Labour leadership speculation grows
events.description: Labour's Andy Burnham won the Makerfield parliamentary by-election on Friday, defeating Reform UK candidate Rob Kenyon and returning to Westminster after years as Manchester mayor. The victory strengthens Burnham's position as a potential successor to Prime Minister Keir Starmer, as speculation grows over Labour's future leadership.

events.title: Colombia sees new peace breakthrough as 100 rebels hand over arms
events.description: About 100 guerrillas surrendered their weapons in southern Colombia on Thursday under a peace deal with leftist President Gustavo Petro. The surrender came days before Sunday's presidential runoff election between Petro ally Senator Ivan Cepeda, who promises to continue the peace initiative, and far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella, who opposes it.

events.title: Obama opens Chicago presidential center, urges Americans to defend democracy
events.description: Former US president Barack Obama opened his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling for the defense of democracy in a star-studded event attended by three former presidents. “I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of how precious our democracy truly is,” Obama said, as Stevie Wonder closed the celebration.

events.title: British 'mafia boss' to be extradited to Spain
events.description: A Scottish 'mafia boss' can be extradited to Spain, a court in Amsterdam ruled.

events.title: As Hungary’s Magyar joins EU summit, sidelined Orban meets with far-right allies

events.title: UK border official and former Hong Kong cop jailed for spying on behalf of China
events.description: A former U.K. border official and a retired Hong Kong police officer have been sentenced for spying on dissidents in Britain

events.title: An ancient oak tree said to have sheltered legendary Robin Hood has died
events.description: The 1,200-year-old Major Oak in Sherwood Forest, linked to the legend of Robin Hood, is believed to have died
events.ticker_label: 💥 An ancient oak tree said to have sheltered legendary Robin Hood has died

events.title: Cambodian Supreme Court upholds incitement conviction of opposition politician in charged case
events.description: Cambodia’s Supreme Court has upheld the incitement conviction of prominent opposition politician Rong Chhun but suspended the remainder of his prison sentence, allowing him to remain free while barring him from politics for years

events.title: A special election in the UK could hasten the rise of Andy Burnham and the end for Keir Starmer
events.description: Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister is on the line in a special election in Makerfield in northwest England

events.title: Putin and leaders of Southeast Asia agree to bolster ties at a summit in Russia
events.description: President Vladimir Putin is hailing Russia’s ties with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations as he hosts a summit intended to strengthen economic and political ties with the bloc

events.title: Peru’s president announces that Pope Leo will visit in early November
events.description: Peruvian President José María Balcázar has announced that Pope Leo XIV will visit Peru in early November

events.title: Macron's diplomatic efforts bring Trump closer to European views
events.description: French President Emmanuel Macron's effort to court U.S. President Donald Trump culminated in a surprise moment at the Versailles Palace

events.title: Labour's Andy Burnham wins special election, setting up showdown to lead Britain
events.description: Labour’s Andy Burnham has won a special election for a seat in Parliament, positioning him to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer

events.title: Greece's Parthenon gets a facelift, revealing a look not seen for 220 years
events.description: Visitors to the Acropolis can now view the Parthenon’s western side in a more complete form after restorers installed two new marble blocks in long-empty gaps on the ancient temple

events.title: Church of England apologizes for role in forced adoptions as recent as the mid-1970s
events.description: The Church of England has apologized for its role in forced adoptions as recent as the mid-1970s

events.title: About 100 Colombian guerrilla dissidents disarm under peace talks with government
events.description: About a hundred Colombian guerrilla dissidents have surrendered their weapons as part of a peace process with President Gustavo Petro's government

events.title: Luigi Mangione bails on psychiatric defense in UnitedHealthcare CEO assassination case
events.description: Luigi Mangione withdraws psychiatric defense in UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson murder case, according to new court filings ahead of trial.

events.title: Luigi Mangione's emotional disturbance defense may have huge impact in his other case: lawyer
events.description: Luigi Mangione's lawyers may use an extreme emotional disturbance defense in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, which could reduce charges at trial.

events.title: Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann kept 'Dexter'-style kill room but wasn't as smart as he thought: DA
events.description: Rex Heuermann had a 'Dexter'-style kill room in his basement with plastic sheets, according to the prosecutor who secured the Gilgo Beach killer's plea.

events.title: Trump personally signs Iran deal in major diplomatic breakthrough and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Florida couple reaches agreement with daughter's biological parents after alleged IVF mix-up
events.description: A Florida couple will raise a genetically unrelated child after an alleged embryo mix-up at a fertility clinic, reaching a custody agreement with the child's biological parents.

events.title: DHS torches Spanberger after illegal immigrant charged with child sex abuse at babysitter's house granted bond
events.description: DHS demands Virginia Gov. Spanberger intervene after illegal immigrant allegedly charged with sexual battery of a 5-year-old girl in Woodbridge.

events.title: New Mexico seeks massive penalty from Meta after jury found tech giant liable for endangering children
events.description: New Mexico seeks nearly $1 billion from Meta after a jury found the tech giant liable for endangering children and misleading the public on safety.

events.title: From bear hugs to handshakes: How India lost its edge with Trump while Pakistan quietly gained ground
events.description: Amid India's push to isolate Pakistan, shifting geopolitics have elevated Islamabad's diplomatic profile. President Trump's meeting with Modi suggests New Delhi is looking to regain momentum.

events.title: Israel launches 'Biblical Highway' amid campaign to erase Jewish history
events.description: Israel's government brands Route 60 as the Biblical Highway, aiming to strengthen heritage tourism and the nation's identity as the land of the Bible.

events.title: 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope
events.description: A 21-year-old woman died after instructors launched her from a bridge without attaching safety ropes during a rope-jumping event near São Paulo, Brazil.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.
